mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, independent of regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 2.63 near cycle-low levels
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and 20d forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive FEDFUNDS declines coincide with negative forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month span, it signals an inflection toward labor-market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest value 5.2 with recent labor market softening
regime_hint: labor market turning point / early slowdown
failure_condition: Triggered instances of UNRATE increases > 0.3pp are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.02) when excluding recession-dated periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a low or falling federal funds rate jointly signal robust consumer demand unconstrained by tight monetary policy, producing a short-term positive equity reaction over the next 5 trading days; the effect requires both conditions jointly since strong sales alone under high rates typically reflect inflation pass-through rather than real demand strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS falling or below 3%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show no statistically significant difference in 5d forward returns versus RSAFS-only trigger instances (interaction term coefficient p > 0.10), falsifying the need for joint conditioning.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating industrial production growth signals supply-side exhaustion and margin compression ahead, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this relationship is regime-specific and should not hold during early-cycle recoveries where INDPRO deceleration reflects normalization rather than exhaustion.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration signals produce no significant negative skew in 20d forward returns during independently-identified late-cycle windows, or the same signal produces positive returns in early-cycle windows with equal or greater magnitude, refuting regime specificity.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects ongoing economic expansion and consumer income support, generating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as a continuation signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and 5d forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above a restrictive threshold of 5%, tightening financial conditions compress valuation multiples and raise recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days) until rates begin easing.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS currently 2.63, well below threshold, establishing baseline for future trigger evaluation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle at restrictive levels
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS > 5.0% show 60d forward SPX returns not significantly different from or greater than periods with FEDFUNDS < 3%, refuting the restrictive-threshold effect.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts occurring simultaneously with a rising federal funds rate signals interest-rate-driven demand destruction in a rate-sensitive sector, foreshadowing broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the interaction is required because falling HOUST alone amid falling rates typically reflects supply-side normalization rather than demand destruction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month decline > 5% AND FEDFUNDS rising over same period
failure_condition: Joint HOUST-decline/FEDFUNDS-rise triggers do not produce more negative 20d forward returns than HOUST-decline-only triggers with statistical significance (p > 0.10), invalidating the interaction requirement.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime (elevated CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth), rising industrial production is interpreted by markets as fueling further inflationary pressure and prompting anticipated monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; in low-inflation regimes the same INDPRO increase signals healthy growth and produces positive returns, making the direction regime-dependent.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month rises AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 4%
failure_condition: INDPRO increases under high-CPI-growth conditions produce forward 60d SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from or greater than those under low-CPI-growth conditions, refuting the regime-conditional sign flip.
